infobox.Guest stars:: Zachary Levi Sam Huntington Jazmyn Simon Ralph Macchio Jimmi Simpson Robert LaSardo Ashley Fliehr Mena Suvari John Cena Emma Tremblay Julianna Guill Carlos Jacott Dexter Bell Nathan Mitchell Dan Payne Mark Acheson Seth Whittaker S.E. Hinton

sections.heading: Intro
sections.text:
Psych: The Movie is the first Psych movie and premiered on USA Network on December 7, 2017. It takes place around three years after the events of the show's finale, " The Break-Up ". Series creator Steve Franks and series star James Roday Rodriguez wrote the script and supervised production, and Franks also directed.
The first news of the film was broken by Cary Elwes , who plays Pierre Despereaux in the series, in April 2017 at Comic Con. However, Cary Elwes himself did unfortunately not appear in the movie.
Unfortunately, Timothy Omundson suffered a stroke right before filming. After the decision was made to go ahead without him, Steve Franks and James Roday Rodriguez "camped out" in a restaurant booth and rewrote the script in 72 hours. Tim still makes an appearance, but only in a very limited role.
Feel Better, Lassie!

sections.heading: Synopsis
sections.text: Shawn, Gus and friends reunite for the holidays when a mystery assailant targets one of their own.

sections.heading: Cast
sections.text:
Returning Cast:
James Roday Rodriguez as Shawn Spencer Dulé Hill as Burton "Gus" Guster Maggie Lawson as Juliet O'Hara Corbin Bernsen as Henry Spencer Kirsten Nelson as Karen Vick Timothy Omundson as Carlton Lassiter Kurt Fuller as Woodrow Strode Sage Brocklebank as Buzz McNab John Cena as Ewan O'Hara Jimmi Simpson as Mary Lightly Mena Suvari as Allison Cowley Ralph Macchio as Nick Conforth
New Cast:
Zachary Levi as William McGoldrick Sam Huntington as Samuel Sloane Jazmyn Simon as Selene Gilmore Robert LaSardo as El Proveedor Emma Tremblay as Iris Vick Julianna Guill as Butterfly McMillan Carlos Jacott as Gus's Boss Dexter Bell as Security Guard Ashley Fliehr as Heather Rockrear Nathan Mitchell as Black Gentleman Ninja Mark Acheson as Beaten Criminal Dan Payne as EP Henchman 1 Unknown actor as EP Henchman 2 Seth Whittaker as EP Henchman 3 Unknown actor as EP Henchman 4 S.E. Hinton as Pony in a Onesie (voice)

sections.heading: Plot Summary
sections.text:
Shawn is wearing a disguise and looking through a bag of jewelry on the San Francisco black market in the hopes of finding the engagement ring that was stolen from him after proposing to Juliet during " The Break-Up ". He is confronted by the market's owner El Proveedor ( Robert LaSardo ) over his suspicious appearance, but manages to escape with a bag of jewelry. He leads his pursuers into the Blitzen Ross building where Gus works, under whose desk he hides successfully. This allows Gus to direct El Proveedor and his men out of the building and away from Shawn. Shawn has still not found the ring, however.
Juliet and her partner Samuel Sloane ( Sam Huntington ) are ready to go home after a productive day of police surveillance when they see a hooded figure commit a theft. Juliet gives chase and confronts the thief, who turns out to be Iris Vick ( Emma Tremblay ), Chief Vick 's daughter. Juliet decides not to arrest her.
Samuel goes home and is there confronted by William McGoldrick ( Zachary Levi ); the two seem to have a past with each other. Samuel pulls out a hidden gun and points it at William, but William says that he no longer works alone. He has his henchpeople, Heather Rockrear ( Ashley Fliehr ) and Black Gentleman Ninja ( Nathan Mitchell ), shoot Samuel.
The next morning, Juliet visits Chief Vick with the intention of telling her about her daughter's crime before driving to work together. When the Chief moves to another room, Iris comes downstairs to explain to Juliet that she committed the theft in order to fit in with a group of bullying classmates and begs her not to tell her mother about it. Juliet concedes and assures her of her emotional support. As Juliet and the Chief leave, Juliet receives the call that Samuel has been admitted to the hospital and is in a coma.
El Proveedor is interrogating a man ( Mark Acheson ) whom he suspects of being connected to Shawn, but isn't getting anywhere. One of his henchmen comes in and tells him that he found a label with Gus's name on the inside of the fake beard that Shawn lost during the chase. El Proveedor orders his men to find Gus immediately.
Shawn is inside the new Psychphrancisco office when Gus enters. The two make plans to visit the planetarium later that day before Shawn tells Gus that Samuel is in the hospital and that they need to visit him.
Shawn and Gus go to the hospital and enter Samuel's room. Chief Vick is informing her officers about Samuel's stolen dongle, a device that gives its user full access to the San Francisco Police Department database. Shawn and Gus offer to help find the culprits, but are kicked out by a frustrated Juliet for their disrespectful behavior during the briefing.
Shawn and Gus exit the hospital and are beckoned over by Nick Conforth ( Ralph Macchio ), their instructor during their short spell at the police academy and also Sam's during his training. He tells them that he's afraid Juliet is hiding something, as Samuel had been receiving several calls from a disposable phone number detailing 'the crimes of Juliet' and was shot shortly after locating the signal. Shawn and Gus promise to investigate.
Shawn and Gus are walking in a nearby forest and find the cell tower the signal originated from. They find out that the only building in its vicinity is a mental hospital and decide to make their way over there.
Shawn and Gus arrive at the hospital and discover it closed two years ago. After subduing a guard dog , they stumble upon a room containing computer equipment with Samuel's dongle plugged into the system. The screens are full of classified police documents, and one of them contains a picture that indicates someone has been spying on Juliet. Shawn and Gus are worried that she might be the criminals' next victim, especially when they come across a document titled "The Crimes of Juliet O'Hara". Suddenly, they are startled by the sound of a door opening.
William and his henchpeople walk into the computer room and sense something is amiss. William orders his underlings to do a perimeter check, but Shawn and Gus manage to escape unseen, with Shawn taking the guard dog, who he names Morrissey, with him. When William sees that someone accessed the computer screen, he informs his team that they've been compromised and will now have to speed things up a little.
Chief Vick is in bed when she hears a sound indicating burglary. She goes to investigate with her gun drawn and is surprised to find Shawn, Gus, and Morrissey waiting for her in the garage. Shawn has decided to give her Morrissey as a Christmas gift, much to the Chief's surprise. When Shawn informs her about the mental hospital and what they found, she agrees to go with them.
Shawn and Gus take the Chief to the mental hospital, but discover that the computer room has been cleared. They are about to investigate further when they encounter Juliet, who learned of police reports involving their car and decided to follow up on it. She tells Shawn and Gus that she doesn't want them to meddle in her affairs and orders them to leave. Juliet then tells the Chief that she is the only one who can fix this mess and tries to convince her to let her handle it on her own. She believes she knows where to start, and Chief Vick lets her investigate.
Shawn and Gus go to the planetarium, where Gus becomes immediately smitten with a fellow space enthusiast Selene ( Jazmyn Simon ) and plans to make her his. However, she makes the first move and invites him up to the telescopes, which kind of throws him off his game. Later, their good time is interrupted by a fight regarding which planet deserves to be the ninth one in our solar system. Selene then suddenly kisses Gus passionately and he reciprocates before storming out of the planetarium in a huff.
Juliet goes back to the hospital to visit Samuel and investigate further, only to discover that he has been murdered. She doesn't see Heather Rockrear get into the elevator behind her.
The next morning, Juliet, Shawn, and Gus are in the morgue to find out from coroner Butterfly McMillan ( Julianna Guill ) and her new assistant Woody how Samuel was murdered. Selene suddenly enters and tries to convince Gus that they are meant for each other, but he is hesitant. She seems to know everything about Gus, even down to his favorite sandwich, and asks him to think about a potential relationship before leaving. Butterfly unexpectedly finds Samuel's dongle in his stomach. They plug it into the computer and discover that the only file that has not been completely erased by the acid is 'The Crimes of Juliet O'Hara'.
Shawn and Gus walk back into the Psych office, where El Proveedor and his henchmen lie in wait. Shawn explains why he stole the bag of jewelry, but El Proveedor orders his henchmen to kill the duo regardless. Shawn quickly intervenes and tells him of his 'psychic' powers. El Proveedor orders him to predict his future through a deck of tarot cards and Shawn makes three random predictions. El Proveedor promises not to kill them now but also assures them that he will be back with a vengeance if Shawn's predictions do not come true.
Shawn and Gus go to Juliet's to help with her investigation. Juliet admits that for each of the cases mentioned in 'The Crimes of Juliet O'Hara', she did not strictly follow police protocol and skipped some steps to get the culprits behind bars sooner, all of whom were hoping to get off on a technicality. However, she is struggling to find out which one of them would have the most reason for revenge. Using his hyperobservancy skills, Shawn is quickly able to deduce that this must be William, who during his time as a criminal informant, provided the police with crucial information on all of the culprits yet was still put away by Juliet. Shawn recalls the picture he saw during his first visit to the mental hospital and now realizes that it must have been taken from the building across the street. Just as they look out to it, William moves into view. He notices them and all four rush downstairs. Juliet confronts William with her gun drawn and he runs back inside. Shawn, Gus and Juliet give chase and appear to have him cornered. William fires warning shots, but says that he promised someone he couldn't kill Juliet yet. He soon backs into an adjacent room and escapes by triggering a big metal door to fall down.
Henry arrives at Juliet's apartment and announces that he has come to help. Juliet is frustrated that he showed up without consulting with her and leaves the room in exasperation. Shawn follows Juliet and turns a listening ear. He tells her he will not let her deal with this situation on her own and assures her that he will be by her side no matter what. Juliet goes outside for some fresh air and face-times Lassiter to receive further emotional support.
The whole house is fast asleep. There is a sound in the kitchen and Shawn wakes with a start. He steps out of the bedroom and finds Mary Lightly ( Jimmi Simpson ), whom Shawn is sure had been killed by Mr. Yin during " Mr. Yin Presents... ". Shawn is confused and realizes he's dreaming once he sees a talking Pony in a Onesie . Shawn expresses his concerns about not being able to find the ring to Mary, who tells him not to worry about it and breaks into "Allison Road" by Gin Blossoms. Shawn joins in but is then rudely awoken by Henry.
Iris is feeling more confident after her talk with Juliet and stands up to the bullies' ring leader over the phone on her way to school. She hangs up delighted but is then kidnapped by William.
Chief Vick, Juliet, Shawn, Gus, Henry, and Woody meet William on a dock, and he takes them to the prison of Alcatraz, which is undergoing restoration. William is about to reveal that he is actually working for Juliet's old enemy Allison Cowley ( Mena Suvari ) when Allison makes her entrance by shooting him. Allison tells the group that she has wanted a rematch against Juliet ever since she was arrested by her during " Yang 3 in 2D " and explains that she recruited William as a pawn once she found out he was plotting his own revenge against Juliet. She points out to Chief Vick that her daughter is somewhere in the building and that there are explosives involved. The Chief runs off in search of Iris, and Allison announces that their showdown has officially begun, with her henchpeople Heather Rockrear and Black Gentleman Ninja having been instructed to take care of the four men in the party.
Shawn and Gus are confronted by Black Gentleman Ninja, who impresses them with his rapier skills. As they face possible death, they discuss their relationship issues, with Gus telling Shawn that he regrets not getting together with Selene when he had the chance, and Shawn echoing his regret by saying he should not have allowed a lost engagement ring to prevent him from marrying Juliet . Black Gentleman Ninja interrupts the conversation by further showcasing his blade mastery, causing Shawn and Gus to run off in fear.
Chief Vick finds her daughter bound and gagged in a locked cell and runs off to find the key.
Elsewhere, Henry and Woody are attacked by Heather Rockrear, who unleashes an array of wrestling moves on them. Both men take quite a beating, but Henry still manages to incapacitate Heather Rockrear by catching one of her legs with his scarf. Chief Vick helps the two finish her off and but quickly resumes her search for the key.
Meanwhile, Juliet is confronted by Allison, and the two start fighting. Allison originally gets the upper hand and points a shotgun at Juliet, who is lying on the ground; however, Juliet kicks up and is able to eventually wrestle the gun away from Allison and knock her out. Chief Vick arrives and grabs the key off of Allison's person.
Shawn and Gus are still running away from Black Gentleman Ninja when he approaches them from the other side of the corridor while keeping a piano wire to Henry's throat. Shawn whispers to Gus to sneak up behind Black Gentleman Ninja and get down on all fours to form a tripping hazard. Gus gets into position and Shawn communicates the plan to Henry in cryptic terms. Henry understands and plays his part. He manages to get free, but Black Gentleman Ninja is too nimble to trip over Gus and is not incapacitated. However, he has no choice but to surrender once Woody arrives holding a shotgun.
The Chief is about to unlock Iris's cell when Juliet reminds her of the explosives, which are wired to the lock. They are struggling with the explosives and time is running out. Iris manages to free her hands and pull the tape off her mouth and tells them that the code to dismantle the bomb is the date Juliet arrested Allison. Juliet remembers that day very well and quickly enters the code. Chief Vick can finally insert the key and frees Iris.
Back on shore, Gus is immediately accosted by Selene. He agrees to a relationship and they kiss. El Proveedor approaches the group and explains that all of Shawn's predictions actually came true. A series of flashbacks then shows that Shawn had enlisted the help of Buzz McNab ( Sage Brocklebank ) to guarantee their accuracy. In return, El Proveedor has hunted down Shawn's engagement ring and now offers his services as an ordained marriage officiant. Shawn and Juliet accept and are joined in matrimony on the spot.
Shawn and Gus are at the Psych office and about to leave for the honeymoon when Ewan O'Hara ( John Cena ) bursts in to congratulate Shawn. He explains he is in a bit of a tight spot and that they need to leave. They run out through the backdoor just as a military team bursts in, heading for their next adventure.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
This film is dedicated to Terry Goldman ( link , link and link ), a friend and colleague of the Psych cast and crew who handled all of the show's social media presence and tragically passed away from a sudden heart attack on September 27, 2017 at age 45. The shirt that Shawn wears in the final scene says "#TeamGrimmie". This is in honor of The Voice and YouTube sensation Christina Grimmie who was tragically shot and killed on June 10, 2016 at age 22 while signing autographs following a concert performance in Orlando. She was a big fan of Psych and a friend of the cast. Shawn notices that Morrissey is a rescue dog from the Tiger Frances Foundation. This is a real foundation that was started by Maggie Lawson in 2015. It is dedicated to finding loving homes for all animals and ending animal abuse ( link ). The opening title card is based on the Marvel Studios flipbook logo. Selene is played by Jazmyn Simon , Dulé Hill 's real-life wife. In the beginning, Shawn is wearing half of the beard disguise he and Gus wore for the characters Soupcan Sam and Satchel Gizmo in S7E1 " Santabarbaratown 2 " and S8E4 " Someone's Got a Woody ". Henry mentions a band called "Oliver & Angus". This is not an actual band but rather a reference to Corbin Bernsen 's two sons. Shawn tells Gus that Juliet would never be in a Hallmark movie. This is an inside joke referring to the 2017 Hallmark movies My Favorite Wedding and Christmas Encore , which both starred Maggie Lawson. At the end of the film, Shawn grabs a pineapple and asks Gus whether he should slice it up for the road. This is a direct call-back to the Psych pilot . Mary Lightly is wearing a racquetball outfit in Shawn's dream. This is a call-back to S3E16 " An Evening with Mr. Yang ", where Shawn suggests racquetball as a new hobby to Mary after Mr. Yang has been caught. He was also wearing the same outfit in " Psych: The Musical " upon meeting Yang. Selene tells Gus that she knows he likes a bakery called Boom Boom Boom Muffins, a store first referred to in S4E4 " The Devil Is in the Details... and the Upstairs Bedroom " when Shawn made a jingle out of the name. The show's love affair with wrestling continues in this film, with both John Cena and Ashley Fliehr among the cast. Beaten Criminal is played by Mark Acheson , who had earlier played Suspect in the pilot. Carlos Jacott , who appears as Gus's Boss, is a Psych writer and also appeared as Terrance in S1E5 " 9 Lives ", Geoffrey in S6E8 " The Tao of Gus ", Mortimer Camp in S8E3 " Remake A.K.A. Cloudy... With a Chance of Improvement " and Hell Hag in S8E9 " A Nightmare on State Street ". Juliet is introduced as a transfer from the Miami Police Department in Season One , but in the film, her dossier includes a certificate from the Santa Barbara Police Academy signed by Chief Vick. It also states that she was educated at the Santa Barbara University. Music: "Little Drummer Boy" by The Dandy Warhols (opening scene) "Merry Christmas (I Don't Want to Fight Tonight" by Ramones (Shawn is being chased through Gus's office) "Christmas Wrapping" by The Waitresses (background song at the planetarium) "Allison Road" by Gin Blossoms (fight scene at Alcatraz)
"Little Drummer Boy" by The Dandy Warhols (opening scene) "Merry Christmas (I Don't Want to Fight Tonight" by Ramones (Shawn is being chased through Gus's office) "Christmas Wrapping" by The Waitresses (background song at the planetarium) "Allison Road" by Gin Blossoms (fight scene at Alcatraz)

sections.heading: Quotes
sections.text:
El Proveedor: [ pulls down Shawn's fake beard only to find another ] Who the hell wears a beard over a beard? Shawn: Who wears a beard-beard? Overachievers. That's who, Jack.
Gus: Nick, you sound paranoid. Shawn: Plus you're mixing horizontal and vertical stripes. Gus: While hiding behind a reindeer and a menorah. Shawn: Hurts your credibility. Gus: It's theologically incongruous.
Shawn: [ when Juliet shows up at the mental hospital unexpectedly ] Jules, we didn't report this. Juliet: No, but you ran over 45 traffic cones, tripped four silent gate alarms and knocked over a Cal-Trans trailer while you were speeding away. Shawn and Gus: [ simultaneously while pointing to the other ] He was driving.
Selene: [ points up at an angle ] That's where it would be, planet number 9. Obviously, if this telescope were powerful enough. Gus: [ chuckles ] I hate to correct you, but [ points up at a different angle ] Pluto's way over there. Selene: Why would I be pointing it at Pluto? Gus: Because you're showing me the ninth planet. Selene: Yes, I'm showing you Eris. Gus: Eris? The dwarf planet? [ laughs ] Eris is stupid. Pluto is, has been and will always be the ninth planet. Selene: Why would we make Pluto a planet again? We've already rectified that mistake. Gus: [ doesn't believe his ears ] Mistake? Hasn't Pluto been through enough already? Selene: [ adamant ] Eris is bigger. Gus: By fifteen miles. By your logic, why don't we make everything a planet? Why don't we make that Nativity Jesus a planet? Selene: Why don't we make your forehead a planet? Gus: What? [ Selene kisses Gus passionately and Gus reciprocates before storming out of the planetarium in a huff ]
Butterfly: Then he got fired for closing up a cadaver with a chili dog in the chest cavity. Woody: Not true, Butterfly. I got fired for opening the cadaver back up to retrieve the chili dog because [ pregnant pause ] I took a working lunch. Gus: Oooooooooohh. Juliet: Wow. Butterfly: Yup.
Gus: [ to Selene at the morgue ] Look, you can't follow me around, research me, try to kiss me, sniff my head, make me feel uncomfortable... Shawn: [ in the other room, to Woody ] He's literally done all those things since Thanksgiving. Woody: He's getting Gussed, no wonder he doesn't like it.
Woody: I'm not crazy about overpowering a woman in a singlet. Henry: She's not wearing a singlet. Woody: No, I am. Under my clothes.
SWAT member: [ from a helicopter outside Psychphrancisco ] Ewan O'Hara, come out with your hands up. Ewan: Guys, this is all about me, you have nothing to worry about. SWAT member: Shawn Spencer and associate, come out as well. Ewan: Okay, now it's about you guys too, could be a problem. Gus: [ indignant ] Associate? I pay the damn rent in this place! Shawn: Maybe if you were around more often they'd know who you were.
